definition.name: quantum_marginal_problem
definition.definition: The quantum marginal problem asks: given k density matrices ρ_1, ..., ρ_k on Hilbert spaces H_{S_1}, ..., H_{S_k} (where S_1, ..., S_k are subsystems of a composite system), does there exist a global density matrix ρ on the full Hilbert space H = ⊗_i H_i such that Tr_{H\S_j}(ρ) = ρ_j for each j? The set of compatible global states is an affinely constrained state space: C = {ρ ∈ S(B(H)) : Tr_{H\S_j}(ρ) = ρ_j, j = 1,...,k}. Each marginal constraint is equivalent to multiple linear constraints on ρ (matching all matrix entries of the reduced density matrix), so m = Σ_j dim(H_{S_j})². The main theorem bounds the commutant dimension (hence GNS structure) of extremal compatible states. Reference: Klyachko, J. Phys. Conf. Ser. 36, 2006.
